description: secretx-file/README.md:32 says 'on Unix the file is created with mode 0600 if it does not exist'. The actual implementation (lib.rs:165-181) writes a NEW temp file with mode 0600 and renames it over any existing destination, so the resulting inode is always 0600 regardless of prior file existence. This is more secure than the README implies (a pre-existing 0644 file gets replaced with a 0600 inode), but the wording suggests existing files keep their prior modes. Either the README is wrong or — if the README intent is the real spec — the implementation is wrong and should preserve existing modes. Pick one and align.
title: [bug] README claims '0600 if it does not exist' but rename always replaces inode
